AUDIT_TOOLS="/usr/bin/auditctl /usr/bin/aureport /usr/bin/ausearch /usr/bin/autrace /usr/sbin/audispd"

# Create AIDE configuration file if it does not exist
if [ ! -f /etc/aide/aide.conf ]; then
    cat << EOF > /etc/aide/aide.conf
include /etc/aide/audit-tools.aide
EOF

# Create the AIDE database file for audit tools if it does not exist

    aide --create-db
fi

# Register each audit tool with AIDE

    # Check if the tool is already registered

    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
        # Add the tool to AIDE configuration file
        echo "file $TOOL flags=md5,perm,own" >> /etc/aide/audit-tools.aide
    fi
done

# Update the AIDE database with the new configuration

#!/bin/bash

# Define AIDE configuration file path
AIDE_CONFIG="/etc/aide.conf"

# Check if AIDE configuration file exists
if [ ! -f "$AIDE_CONFIG" ]; then
    echo "AIDE configuration file not found."
    exit 1
fi

# Read all lines from the AIDE configuration file
while IFS= read -r line; do
    # Check if the line contains 'file' keyword and does not have 'ignorexattr'
    if [[ $line == *'file'* && ! $line =~ ignorexattr ]]; then
        # Add 'ignorexattr = no' to enable extended attribute checking
        sed -i '/^'$line'/a ignorexattr = no' "$AIDE_CONFIG"
    fi
done < "$AIDE_CONFIG"

# Restart AIDE to apply the changes
systemctl restart aide

# Set FIPS mode to enforced
setenforce 1

# Install required packages for FIPS compliance

# Configure SSH to use FIPS compliant algorithms

echo "Ciphers aes256-ctr,arcfour128,aes192-ctr" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config

#!/bin/bash

# Load the system-wide cryptographic policy
CRYPTO_POLICY="/etc/crypto-policies/backends/ssh-server.conf"

# Check if the crypto-policy file exists and is readable
if [[ -r "$CRYPTO_POLICY" ]]; then
    # Read the cipher specification from the system policy
    CIPHER_SPEC=$(grep 'Ciphers' "$CRYPTO_POLICY" | awk '{print $2}' | tr -d '#')
    
    # Check if the cipher specification is uncommented and valid
    if [[ -n "$CIPHER_SPEC" ]]; then
        # Update SSH server configuration to use the system policy ciphers
        sed -i '/^Ciphers/s/^.*$/Ciphers '$CIPHER_SPEC'/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
        
        # Restart SSH service to apply changes
        systemctl restart sshd
    else
        echo "Cipher specification not found in $CRYPTO_POLICY"
    fi
else
    echo "$CRYPTO_POLICY does not exist or is not readable"
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Load system cryptographic policy
CRYPTO_POLICY=$(cryptsetup is-fips-enabled)

# Check if FIPS mode is enabled
if [ "$CRYPTO_POLICY" = "1" ]; then
    # Set SSH server to use only FIPS 140-2 validated MACs
    sed -i '/^MACs/s:.*:hmac-sha2-256,hmac-sha2-512:' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
else
    # If FIPS mode is not enabled, set SSH server to default MACs
    sed -i '/^MACs/s:.*: hmac-md5,hmac-sha1,hmac-ripemd160,hmac-sha2-256,hmac-sha2-512:' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
fi

# Restart SSH service to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

# Check if automatic graphical login is enabled
AUTOMATIC_LOGIN=$(gsettings get org.gnome.login-screen enable-auto-login)

    gsettings set org.gnome.login-screen enable-auto-login false

ProtectSystem=full
PrivateDevices=true
NoNewPrivileges=true
EOF

# Reload systemd manager configuration to apply the changes
systemctl daemon-reload

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure gsettings is available
if ! command -v gsettings &> /dev/null; then
    echo "gsettings could not be found, please install gnome-desktop-environs"
    exit 1
fi

# Set mandatory screen lock on screensaver activation
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 0
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-enabled true
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-on-suspend false
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-on-hibernate false
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-on-logout false

# Ensure the settings persist across reboots by creating a systemd service
cat << EOF | sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/gnome-screensaver-lock.service
[Unit]
Description=GNOME Screensaver Lock on Activation

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/bin/bash -c 'gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 0 && gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-enabled true && gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-on-suspend false && gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-on-hibernate false && gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-on-logout false'

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

# Enable and start the service to ensure it runs on boot
sudo systemctl enable gnome-screensaver-lock.service
sudo systemctl start gnome-screensaver-lock.service

# Set the default timeout to 5 minutes (300 seconds)
SUDO_TIMEOUT=300

# Check if the sudoers file exists

#!/bin/bash

# Set the default timeout to 5 minutes (300 seconds)
SUDO_TIMEOUT=300

# Check if the sudoers file exists
if [ ! -f /etc/sudoers ]; then
    echo "sudoers file not found."

#!/bin/bash

# Define the password change command
change_password() {
    echo "Changing password for $1..."
    passwd "$1"
}

# Check if the user exists and is a system user
if ! id "$1" &>/dev/null; then
    echo "User $1 does not exist."
    exit 1
fi

# Get the current password hash
current_hash=$(echo -n "$1:$2" | sha512sum | cut -d ' ' -f 1)

# Function to check if a new password meets the criteria
check_password_quality() {
    local old_password="$1"
    local new_password="$2"

    # Calculate the number of different characters
    local diff_chars=$(echo "$old_password" | grep -o . | sort | uniq | wc -l)
    local common_chars=$(tr -cd '[:alnum:]' <<<"$new_password" | tr -cd '[:alnum:]' <<<"$old_password" | sort | uniq -d | wc -l)

    # Check if the number of different characters is at least 8
    if [ $((diff_chars - common_chars)) -ge 8 ]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Read new password from user input
read -s -p "Enter new password: " new_password

# Check the quality of the new password
if check_password_quality "$2" "$new_password"; then
    # Update the password hash
    echo "$1:$new_password" | chpasswd
    echo "Password changed successfully."
else
    echo "New password does not meet the criteria. Please try again."
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Load current password policy settings
current_policy=$(grep 'password_quality' /etc/login.defs)

# Check if the policy already exists and adjust accordingly
if [[ $current_policy =~ ^\s*password_quality\s*=\s*(on|yes|1)$ ]]; then
    # Policy is already enabled, check for consecutive character limit setting
    current_limit=$(grep 'max_consecutive_chars' /etc/login.defs)
    if [[ ! $current_limit =~ ^\s*max_consecutive_chars\s*=\s*4$ ]]; then
        # Set the max consecutive characters to 4 if not already set
        sed -i '/password_quality/s/^.*$/&\nmax_consecutive_chars = 4/' /etc/login.defs
    fi
else
    # Enable password quality and set max consecutive characters to 4
    echo 'password_quality = on' >> /etc/login.defs
    echo 'max_consecutive_chars = 4' >> /etc/login.defs
fi

# Apply the changes by running chage command
chage --show /root

#!/bin/bash

# Define character classes
upper=$(echo 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ')
lower=$(echo 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz')
digits=$(echo '0123456789')
special=$(echo '!@#$%^&*()-_=+[]{}|;:,.<>?')

# Function to check password complexity
check_password_complexity() {
    local password="$1"
    local count=0

    [[ $password =~ [A-Z] ]] && ((count++))
    [[ $password =~ [a-z] ]] && ((count++))
    [[ $password =~ [0-9] ]] && ((count++))
    [[ $password =~ [[:punct:]] ]] && ((count++))

    if (( count < 4 )); then
        return 1 # Password does not meet complexity requirements
    fi

    return 0 # Password meets complexity requirements
}

# Check and enforce password policy for all users
for user in $(getent passwd | cut -d: -f1); do
    echo "Checking password complexity for $user..."
    if ! check_password_complexity "$(passwd -S "$user" | grep 'Password last changed:')"; then
        echo "Updating password complexity for $user..."

done

fi

#!/bin/bash

# Load system configuration
source /etc/login.defs

# Set minimum rounds to 5000 for SHA-512 hashing algorithm
min_rounds=5000
max_rounds=$min_rounds

# Update the password hash min/max rounds in the system configuration files
sed -i "s/^PASS_MIN_LEN.*/PASS_MIN_LEN $min_rounds/" /etc/login.defs
sed -i "s/^PASS_MAX_DAYS.*/PASS_MAX_DAYS 99999/" /etc/login.defs
sed -i "s/^PASS_WARN_AGE.*/PASS_WARN_AGE 7/" /etc/login.defs
sed -i "s/^PASS_RESET_TIME.*/PASS_RESET_TIME 0/" /etc/login.defs
sed -i "s/^SHA_MIN_ROUNDS.*/SHA_MIN_ROUNDS $min_rounds/" /etc/login.defs
sed -i "s/^SHA_MAX_ROUNDS.*/SHA_MAX_ROUNDS $max_rounds/" /etc/login.defs

# Update the shadow password file to reflect the new settings
echo "*:*:5000:0:99999:7:::" > /etc/shadow
chown root:root /etc/shadow

#!/bin/bash

# Set maximum password age to 365 days
MAX_PASSWORD_AGE=365

# Loop through all existing local user accounts
for user in $(getent passwd | cut -d: -f1); do
    # Get current password max age for the user
    CURRENT_MAX_AGE=$(chage -l "$user" | grep "Max password age" | awk '{print $4}')

    # Check if the user's max password age is not set to 365 days
    if [[ -z "$CURRENT_MAX_AGE" || "$CURRENT_MAX_AGE" != "$MAX_PASSWORD_AGE" ]]; then
        # Set the maximum password age for the user to 365 days
        chage -M $MAX_PASSWORD_AGE "$user"
    fi
done

#!/bin/bash

# Get all users except root and system accounts
users=$(getent passwd | cut -d: -f1 | grep -vE '^(root|bin|daemon|adm|lp|mail|news|uucp|proxy|www-data|backup|operator|games|man|guest|nobody)')

for user in $users; do
    # Get the last password change time for each user
    last_change=$(chage -l "$user" | grep 'Last Password Change' | awk '{print $3, $4, $5}')
    
    # Check if the last change is less than 1 day ago
    if [[ $(date +%s) -lt $(date -d"$last_change" +%s -d"-1 day") ]]; then
        # Set the minimum password age to 1 day
        chage -d0 "$user"
    fi
done

#!/bin/bash

# Get all users except root and system accounts
users=$(getent passwd | cut -d: -f1 | grep -vE '^(root|bin|daemon|adm|lp|mail|news|uucp|proxy|www-data|backup|operator|games|man|guest|nobody)')

for user in $users; do
    # Get the last password change time for each user
    last_change=$(chage -l "$user" | grep 'Last Password Change' | awk '{print $3, $4, $5}')
    
    # Check if the last change is less than 1 day ago
    if [[ $(date +%s) -lt $(date -d"$last_change" +%s -d"-1 day") ]]; then
        # Set the minimum password age to 1 day
        chage -d0 "$user"
    fi
done
```

#!/bin/bash

# Get all local interactive users
users=$(getent passwd | grep -E '^(daemon|bin|sys|adm|uucp|games|man|lp|mail|news|nobody|ftp|abrt)|/home/' | cut -d: -f1)

for user in $users; do
    # Get the primary group of the user
    primary_group=$(id "$user" --group | head -n 1)
    
    # Get all files and directories within the home directory of the user
    home_dir="/home/$user"
    find "$home_dir" -type f -exec chgrp "$primary_group" {} \;
done

#!/bin/bash

# Get all local interactive users
users=$(getent passwd | grep -E '^(daemon|bin|sys|adm|uucp|games|man|lp|mail|news|nobody|ftp|abrt)|/home/' | cut -d: -f1)

for user in $users; do
    # Get the primary group of the user
    primary_group=$(id "$user" --group | head -n 1)
    
    # Get all files and directories within the home directory of the user
    home_dir="/home/$user"
    find "$home_dir" -type f -exec chgrp "$primary_group" {} \;
done

#!/bin/bash

for user in $(cut -d: -f1 /etc/passwd); do
    if [ "$user" != "root" ]; then
        home_dir=$(eval echo ~$user)
        find "$home_dir" -type f ! -name '.*' -exec chmod 0640 {} \; -o \
          find "$home_dir" -type d ! -name '.*' -exec chmod 0750 {} \;
    fi
done

#!/bin/bash

# Define the minimum permission level for dot-files
MIN_PERM=0740

# Loop through all users including root
for user in $(getent passwd | cut -d: -f1); do
    # Skip if it's not a human user or if the home directory doesn't exist
    if [[ "$user" != "root" && ! -d "/home/$user" ]]; then
        continue
    fi

    # Loop through all dot-files in the home directory
    for file in $(find /home/"$user" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '.*'); do
        # Get current permissions
        current_perm=$(stat -c "%a" "$file")

        # If current permissions are more permissive than MIN_PERM, change them
        if [ "$current_perm" -gt "$MIN_PERM" ]; then
            chmod "$MIN_PERM" "$file"
        fi
    done
done

    for file in $(find /home/"$user" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '.*'); do
        # Get current permissions
        current_perm=$(stat -c "%a" "$file")

        # If current permissions are more permissive than MIN_PERM, change them
        if [ "$current_perm" -gt "$MIN_PERM" ]; then
            chmod "$MIN_PERM" "$file"
        fi
    done
done
```

# Define the sysctl setting to disable legacy virtual syscall mechanism
SYSCTL_SETTING="kernel.vsyscall=0"

# Update all currently installed kernels
for kernel in /boot/vmlinuz-*; do
    if [[ -f "$kernel" ]]; then
        # Extract the version number from the kernel file name
        version=$(basename "$kernel" .el8v*)
        # Create a new boot configuration file for this kernel version
        cat << EOF > "/etc/sysctl.d/10-vsyscall-disable-$version.conf"
$SYSCTL_SETTING
EOF
    fi
done

sysctl -p /etc/sysctl.d/10-vsyscall-disable-*.conf

sed -i '/GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT/s/"$/ $SYSCTL_SETTING"/' /etc/default/grub

#!/bin/bash

# Define function to apply mount options
apply_mount_options() {
    local mount_point=$1
    local fs_type=$2
    local opts=$3

    # Check if filesystem is already mounted and has the required options
    if grep -qE "^$mount_point " /proc/mounts && ! grep -qE "(^|,)noexec,nosuid,nodev($)" "/proc/mounts | grep -w $fs_type" ; then
        return 0 # Already has correct options, do nothing
    fi

    # Apply mount options to the filesystem
    if mountpoint -q "$mount_point"; then
        echo "Mounting $mount_point with options $opts"
        mount -o remount,$opts "$mount_point"
    else
        echo "Skipping unmounted filesystem: $mount_point"
    fi
}

# Get all non-root local partitions
partitions=$(df --local --exclude /proc --exclude /sys --exclude /dev --exclude tmpfs --output=source / | sort -u)

# Loop through each partition and apply the mount options
for partition in $partitions; do
    # Determine filesystem type
    fs_type=$(blkid -o value -s TYPE "$partition")

    # Apply noexec, nosuid, nodev options to non-root local partitions
    if [ "$fs_type" != "swap" ]; then
        apply_mount_options "$partition" "$fs_type" "noexec,nosuid,nodev"
    fi
done

# Update /etc/fstab with the new mount options for persistence
for partition in $partitions; do
    # Determine filesystem type
    fs_type=$(blkid -o value -s TYPE "$partition")

    # Skip swap partitions and already mounted filesystems
    if [ "$fs_type" != "swap" ] && ! grep -qE "^$partition " /proc/mounts; then
        # Get existing mount options from fstab
        current_opts=$(grep "^$partition " /etc/fstab | awk '{print $4}')

        # Add noexec, nosuid, nodev to existing options if not already present
        new_opts="${current_opts:+${current_opts},}noexec,nosuid,nodev"
        sed -i "s|^$partition.*|& $new_opts|" /etc/fstab
    fi
done

    echo "Free-page poisoning has been added to the boot loader command line."

#!/bin/bash

# Define the kernel parameter for object-poisoning
PARAMETER="objectpoison=1"

# Check if the parameter is already set in /etc/sysctl.conf
if grep -q "$PARAMETER" /etc/sysctl.conf; then
    echo "Parameter $PARAMETER is already set."
else
    # Add the parameter to /etc/sysctl.conf
    echo "$PARAMETER" >> /etc/sysctl.conf

    # Apply the sysctl settings immediately
    sysctl -p
fi

# Create a file in /boot that will be used to pass the kernel parameter at boot time
if [ ! -f /boot/objectpoison ]; then
    touch /boot/objectpoison
    chmod 644 /boot/objectpoison
fi

# Add the parameter to the GRUB configuration for all installed kernels
for i in /boot/vmlinuz-*; do
    if [[ $i == *"-vmlinuz"* ]]; then
        # Extract the kernel version from the filename
        KERNEL_VERSION=$(echo "$i" | sed 's/.*-vmlinuz-\([^0-9]*\).*/\1/')
        
        # Check if the parameter is already set in the GRUB configuration for this kernel
        if grep -q "objectpoison=1" "/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"; then
            echo "Parameter $PARAMETER is already set for all kernels."
        else
            # Update the GRUB configuration to include the parameter
            grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg
        fi
    fi
done

# Ensure that the sysctl settings are applied on boot by creating a systemd drop-in file
if [ ! -d "/etc/systemd/system/sysctl.service.d/" ]; then
    mkdir -p "/etc/systemd/system/sysctl.service.d/"
fi

cat << EOF > /etc/systemd/system/sysctl.service.d/objectpoison.conf
[Service]
ExecStart=/sbin/sysctl -p
EOF

# Reload the systemd manager configuration to apply the drop-in file
systemctl daemon-reload

#!/bin/bash

# Install SELinux File Contexts if not already installed
if ! rpm -q selinux-file-contexts-utils > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    yum install -y selinux-file-contexts-utils
fi

# Ensure SELinux is enabled and enforcing mode is set
if ! getenforce | grep -q Enforcing; then
    setenforce 1
fi

# Install AppArmor if not already installed
if ! rpm -q apparmor > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    yum install -y apparmor
fi

# Enable and start AppArmor
systemctl enable apparmor
systemctl start apparmor

# Ensure AppArmor profiles are loaded
for profile in $(apparmor_status | grep 'is not' | awk '{print $3}'); do
    if ! apparmor_parser -R /etc/apparmor.d/$profile; then
        echo "Failed to reload AppArmor profile: $profile"
    fi
done

# Ensure SELinux is enforcing and AppArmor is active
if getenforce | grep -q Enforcing && systemctl is-active --quiet apparmor; then
    exit 0
else
    exit 1
fi

# Define an associative array for time sources and their maximum polling intervals
declare -A NTP_SERVERS=( ["time.nist.gov"]="8" ["pool.ntp.org"]="8" ["time.google.com"]="8" )

# Read current configuration from /etc/ntp.conf
current_servers=$(grep 'server' /etc/ntp.conf | awk '{print $2}' | sort)

# Iterate over the defined NTP servers and ensure they are in the configuration with the correct polling interval
for server in "${!NTP_SERVERS[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -q "server $server" /etc/ntp.conf; then
        # Server not found, add it with the maximum polling interval
        echo "server $server maxpoll ${NTP_SERVERS[$server]}" >> /etc/ntp.conf
    else
        # Server is present, check if the maxpoll setting is correct
        existing_maxpoll=$(grep "server $server" /etc/ntp.conf | awk '{print $4}' | cut -d' ' -f1)
        if [ "$existing_maxpoll" != "${NTP_SERVERS[$server]}" ]; then
            # Maxpoll setting is incorrect, update it
            sed -i "s/server $server maxpoll .*/server $server maxpoll ${NTP_SERVERS[$server]}/g" /etc/ntp.conf
        fi
    fi
done

# Ensure the time daemon is restarted to apply changes
systemctl restart ntpd

# Define critical SSH files and directories
critical_files=(
  "/home/$USER/.ssh/authorized_keys"
  "/home/$USER/.ssh/config"
  "/home/$USER/.ssh/id_rsa"
  "/home/$USER/.ssh/id_rsa.pub"
)

# Check for world-writable permissions on critical SSH files
for file in "${critical_files[@]}"; do
  if [ -w "$file" ] && ! [ -x "$file" ]; then
    chmod go-w "$file"
    chown "$USER:$USER" "$file"
  fi

if [ "$(stat -c '%U' /home/$USER/.ssh)" != "$USER" ] || [ "$(stat -c '%G' /home/$USER/.ssh)" != "root" ]; then

#!/bin/bash

# Check if SSH server configuration file exists
if [ ! -f /etc/ssh/sshd_config ]; then
    echo "SSH server configuration file does not exist."
    exit 1
fi

# Backup the original sshd_config file
cp /etc/ssh/sshd_config /etc/ssh/sshd_config.bak

# Read the current SSH server configuration
while IFS= read -r line; do
    # Check if the line contains 'LoginGraceTime'
    if [[ $line == *LoginGraceTime* ]]; then
        # Replace with the new value
        echo "LoginGraceTime 10" > /etc/ssh/sshd_config
    elif [[ $line == *PrintLastLogins* ]]; then
        # Check if the line is already set to yes, if not add it
        if [[ ! $line =~ yes ]]; then
            echo "$line yes" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config
        fi
    else
        # Write the original line back to the configuration file
        echo "$line" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config
    fi
done < /etc/ssh/sshd_config

# Restart SSH service to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

#!/bin/bash

# Load SSH configuration file path
SSH_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"

# Check if SSH configuration file exists
if [ ! -f "$SSH_CONFIG" ]; then
    echo "SSH configuration file not found."
    exit 1
fi

# Backup the original SSH configuration file
if [ -f "${SSH_CONFIG}.bak" ]; then
    mv "${SSH_CONFIG}.bak" "${SSH_CONFIG}.bak$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
else
    cp "$SSH_CONFIG" "${SSH_CONFIG}.bak"
fi

# Set session key renegotiation limits
sed -i '/^KeyRegenerationInterval/ s/^.*$/KeyRegenerationInterval 3600/' "$SSH_CONFIG"
sed -i '/^ClientAliveInterval/ s/^.*$/ClientAliveInterval 3600/' "$SSH_CONFIG"
sed -i '/^ClientAliveCountMax/ s/^.*$/ClientAliveCountMax 3/' "$SSH_CONFIG"
sed -i '/^ServerKeyBits/ s/^.*$/ServerKeyBits 2048/' "$SSH_CONFIG"

# Set the maximum amount of data before key renegotiation
sed -i '/^MaxSessionTasks/ s/^.*$/MaxSessionTasks 1/' "$SSH_CONFIG"

# Restart SSH service to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

echo "SSH server has been hardened with session key renegotiation limits."

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure /dev/urandom is used for SSH server entropy source
sed -i '/^UseDNS/s/^/#/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
echo 'UseDNS no' >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config

# Generate and set a high-entropy seed for the random-number generator
dd if=/dev/urandom of=/var/lib/ssh/ssh_host_ecdsa_key_new bs=32 count=1 2>/dev/null
mv /var/lib/ssh/ssh_host_ecdsa_key_new /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ecdsa_key

# Regenerate SSH host keys with the new entropy source
ssh-keygen -t ecdsa -b 256 -f /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ecdsa_key -N ''

# Restart SSH service to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

current_policy=$(usbguard status --json)

# Extract connected devices from the policy
connected_devices=$(echo "$current_policy" | jq -r '.devices[] | select(.connection_state == "connected") | .device_id')

# Create a new policy file if it does not exist
if [ ! -f /etc/usbguard/usbguard.conf ]; then

for device in $connected_devices; do

done

if ! systemctl is-active --quiet usbguard; then

systemctl ensure-started auditd

# Define the shadow password file path

if [ ! -f "$SHADOW_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Shadow file does not exist: $SHADOW_FILE"
    exit 1
fi

# Create a unique key for user/group modification tracking
KEY="user_group_modification"

    # Add the write and attribute-change event monitoring rule with the specific key
    echo "-a always,exit -F path=$SHADOW_FILE -F perm=wa -F key=$KEY" >> /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules
fi

# Apply the audit rules
auditctl -R /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules

# Ensure the audit subsystem is configured to watch for write and attribute-change events on the shadow password file
echo "w" > "/proc/sys/fs/auditrune"

#!/bin/bash

# Check if audit system is already configured for disk full action
if grep -qE 'action_mail_acct = root' /etc/audit/auditd.conf; then
    exit 0 # Configuration already exists
fi

# Set up the disk full action to switch to single-user mode
echo "action_mail_acct = root" >> /etc/audit/auditd.conf
echo "disk_full_action = 1" >> /etc/audit/auditd.conf
echo "disk_space_low_action = 1" >> /etc/audit/auditd.conf

# Ensure audit rules are reloaded to apply changes
systemctl restart auditd

# Create a systemd service unit file for persistent single-user mode on disk full
cat <<EOF | tee /etc/systemd/system/disk-full-single-user.service
[Unit]
Description=Switch to single-user mode on disk full
ConditionPathExists=!/var/log/audit/audit.log

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/sbin/telinit 1

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

# Enable the service to start on boot
systemctl enable disk-full-single-user.service

generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Define the audit log directory and threshold percentage
AUDIT_LOG_DIR="/var/log/audit"
THRESHOLD=25

# Calculate the free space in the audit log directory
FREE_SPACE=$(df -k "$AUDIT_LOG_DIR" | tail -1 | awk '{print $4}')

# Get the total space of the audit log directory
TOTAL_SPACE=$(df -k "$AUDIT_LOG_DIR" | tail -1 | awk '{print $2}')

# Calculate the threshold value in kilobytes
THRESHOLD_VALUE=$((TOTAL_SPACE * THRESHOLD / 100))

# Check if free space is below the threshold
if [ "$FREE_SPACE" -lt "$THRESHOLD_VALUE" ]; then
    # Trigger low-space notification action
    auditctl -w "$AUDIT_LOG_DIR" -p warx -k audit_space_low
fi

exit 0

systemctl restart auditd

AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit"

# Loop through each file in the audit rules directory
for file in "$AUDIT_RULES_DIR"/*.rules; do
  # Check if the file exists and has the correct permissions
  if [ -f "$file" ] && [ ! -r "$file" ] || [ ! -w "$file" ]; then
    # Set the file permissions to 0600
    chmod 600 "$file"
  fi
done

#!/bin/bash

# Define the audit rule files directory
AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit"

# Loop through each file in the audit rules directory
for file in "$AUDIT_RULES_DIR"/*.rules; do
  # Check if the file exists and has the correct permissions
  if [ -f "$file" ] && [ ! -r "$file" ] || [ ! -w "$file" ]; then
    # Set the file permissions to 0600
    chmod 600 "$file"
  fi
done
